meanings.meaning: 始终如一地；一贯地；总是
meanings.usage: 用来描述几乎每次都会发生的情况，语气比always更正式
强调时间、数量等的准确性，可用于加强语气
常与介词to搭配，指某个重大事件的开端或预兆
用来描述事先没有预料到的情况，通常修饰事件、问题等名词
可指重大的灾难，也可用于幽默地指代麻烦的小事
既可以作及物动词也可以作不及物动词，强调破碎的剧烈程度

meanings.meaning: 陶器；瓦器；餐具（杯、盘等的统称）
meanings.usage: 不可数名词，通常指陶瓷材质的餐具
常与介词with搭配，指交通工具、人或物体相互碰撞
不可数名词，常用搭配get into a panic表示陷入恐慌
既可以作动词表示刹车的动作，也可作名词指刹车装置
英式英语表达，美式英语常用windshield
用来修饰表示吃、拿等动作的动词，强调急切、过量的状态

meanings.meaning: 狼吞虎咽地吃；吞没；毁灭
meanings.usage: 强调吃东西的速度快、量大，也可引申指吞并、耗尽
常修饰动物，也可指零散的物品或人
不可数名词，可指场景的混乱，也可指人思想上的困惑

phrases.usage: 表示事态、情绪等失去控制，无法被管理或约束

examples.en: The protest got out of control and turned into a riot.
examples.zh: 抗议活动失去了控制，演变成了骚乱。

examples.en: If you don't set rules for the kids, they'll soon get out of control.
examples.zh: 如果你不给孩子们定规矩，他们很快就会无法无天。

phrases.phrase: set up a chain of reactions
phrases.usage: 指一个事件引发一连串连锁反应，常用来描述因果递进的事件序列

examples.en: The announcement of the price rise set up a chain of reactions in the market.
examples.zh: 涨价的公告在市场上引发了一连串连锁反应。

examples.en: A small mistake in the experiment set up a chain of reactions that ruined the whole project.
examples.zh: 实验中的一个小错误引发了连锁反应，毁了整个项目。

phrases.usage: 口语常用表达，意思是照看、留意、密切关注

examples.en: Could you keep an eye on my suitcase while I go to the restroom?
examples.zh: 我去洗手间的时候你能帮我照看一下行李箱吗？

examples.en: We need to keep an eye on the weather forecast in case it rains during the picnic.
examples.zh: 我们得留意天气预报，以防野餐的时候下雨。

phrases.usage: 表示使某人难过/委屈到哭，强调事件对人情绪的强烈影响

examples.en: The cruel comments on her speech reduced her to tears.
examples.zh: 对她演讲的刻薄评论把她弄哭了。

examples.en: The pressure of being a new nurse reduced her to tears several times in the first month.
examples.zh: 作为新护士的压力让她第一个月好几次都哭了。

phrases.usage: 指城市早晚的交通高峰时段，不可数名词短语

examples.en: It's better to leave before 7 a.m. to avoid the rush hour traffic.
examples.zh: 最好早上7点前出发，避开高峰时段的车流。

examples.en: The subway is always crowded during rush hour.
examples.zh: 地铁在高峰时段总是很拥挤。

phrases.usage: 表示陷入恐慌状态，强调突然产生的慌乱情绪

examples.en: She got into a panic when she found her passport was missing.
examples.zh: 她发现护照不见了的时候陷入了恐慌。

examples.en: Don't get into a panic when you meet difficulties, take a deep breath and think of solutions.
examples.zh: 遇到困难时别慌，深呼吸想想解决办法。

phrases.usage: 指车辆等驶到旁边、并排停靠

examples.en: A police car drew up alongside us and signaled us to pull over.
examples.zh: 一辆警车开到我们旁边，示意我们靠边停车。

examples.en: The taxi drew up alongside the curb to pick up the passenger.
examples.zh: 出租车开到路边停靠，接乘客上车。

phrases.usage: 指车辆或司机停车、放慢车速停下

examples.en: He pulled up in front of the gate and got out of the car quickly.
examples.zh: 他在大门前停下车，快速从车里出来。

examples.en: The bus pulled up just as I was running to the stop.
examples.zh: 我正跑向公交站的时候，公交车刚好停下了。

phrases.usage: 表示堆积、积压，可指具体物品的堆叠，也可指事务、问题的累积

examples.en: Work always piles up at the end of the year.
examples.zh: 年底的时候工作总是堆成山。

examples.en: Snow piled up against the windows during the blizzard.
examples.zh: 暴风雪期间，雪靠着窗户堆了起来。

phrases.usage: 表示使某事重新运转、恢复正常运行

examples.en: The new manager tried his best to get the delayed project on the move.
examples.zh: 新经理尽全力让延误的项目重新运转起来。

examples.en: We need extra workers to get the production line on the move after the breakdown.
examples.zh: 故障之后我们需要额外的工人让生产线重新运转。

grammar.title: as if引导的虚拟语气
grammar.definition: as if/though引导的从句如果表示与事实相反的情况，需要使用虚拟语气，用来描述假设、想象或者不真实的场景
grammar.structure: 主句 + as if + 从句（从句动词用过去式表示与现在事实相反，用过去完成式表示与过去事实相反）
grammar.usage: 当说话人认为从句所述内容不真实或者可能性很低时使用虚拟语气，如果所述内容是真实的则用陈述语气

examples.en: It is as if a single unimportant event set up a chain of reactions.
examples.zh: 就好像一件无关紧要的小事引发了一连串的连锁反应。

examples.en: He talks as if he knew everything about the plan, but actually he knows nothing.
examples.zh: 他说得好像他对这个计划了如指掌，但实际上他什么都不知道。

examples.en: She looked as if she had heard some terrible news.
examples.zh: 她看起来好像听到了什么可怕的消息。

grammar.title: 现在分词作伴随/结果状语
grammar.definition: 现在分词（doing形式）可以放在句子末尾，用作状语，表示主句动作发生时伴随的状态，或者主句动作带来的自然结果
grammar.structure: 主句 + , + 现在分词短语
grammar.usage: 现在分词的逻辑主语必须和主句的主语一致，当表示结果时，通常是主句动作自然而然引发的后果

examples.en: The baby pulls the tablecloth off the table, smashing half your best crockery and cutting himself in the process.
examples.zh: 婴儿把桌布从桌子上扯了下来，打碎了你一半的上好餐具，还在这个过程中割伤了自己。

examples.en: He sat by the window, reading a novel attentively.
examples.zh: 他坐在窗边，专心致志地读着一本小说。

examples.en: The heavy rain lasted for three days, causing serious floods in the area.
examples.zh: 大雨持续了三天，在这个地区造成了严重的洪灾。

grammar.title: what引导的名词性从句作主语
grammar.definition: what可以引导主语从句，在从句中充当主语、宾语等成分，整个从句在主句中作主语，通常表示“...的事情/情况”
grammar.structure: What + 从句 + 主句谓语 + 其他成分
grammar.usage: 相当于the thing that的含义，用来指代具体的某件事或某种情况，让句子结构更紧凑

examples.en: What invariably happens is that a great number of things choose to go wrong at precisely the same moment.
examples.zh: 经常发生的情况是，好多事情偏偏都凑在同一时间出问题。

examples.en: What you need to do right now is to calm down and explain the situation clearly.
examples.zh: 你现在需要做的就是冷静下来，把情况解释清楚。

examples.en: What he said at the meeting surprised all the participants.
examples.zh: 他在会上说的话让所有参会者都很惊讶。

sentencePatterns.pattern: We have all experienced days when everything goes wrong.

original.en: We have all experienced days when everything goes wrong.
original.zh: 我们都有过事事不顺心的日子。

imitations.en: We have all experienced moments when we want to give up but choose to hold on in the end.
imitations.zh: 我们都有过想要放弃但最终选择坚持的时刻。

imitations.en: Many people have experienced periods when they feel lost and don't know what to do next.
imitations.zh: 很多人都有过感到迷茫、不知道下一步该做什么的阶段。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is as if + 从句（虚拟语气）

original.en: It is as if a single unimportant event set up a chain of reactions.
original.zh: 就好像一件无关紧要的小事引发了一连串的连锁反应。

imitations.en: It is as if the whole world had forgotten about this small village hidden in the mountains.
imitations.zh: 就好像整个世界都忘记了这个藏在山里的小村庄。

imitations.en: He acted as if nothing had happened, even though he knew he made a big mistake.
imitations.zh: 他表现得就好像什么都没发生一样，尽管他知道自己犯了大错。

sentencePatterns.pattern: As if this were not enough to + 动词短语, + 另一糟糕情况

original.en: As if this were not enough to reduce you to tears, your husband arrives, unexpectedly bringing three guests to dinner.
original.zh: 好像这一切还不足以让你急哭，你丈夫回来了，出乎意料地带了三位客人来家里吃饭。

imitations.en: As if this were not enough to make him anxious, his boss called to say he needed to work extra hours this weekend.
imitations.zh: 好像这还不足以让他焦虑，他的老板又打电话来说他这周末需要加班。

imitations.en: As if this were not enough to ruin the trip, it started pouring with rain when we just arrived at the foot of the mountain.
imitations.zh: 好像这还不足以毁掉这次旅行，我们刚到山脚下的时候就开始下倾盆大雨了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Only + 名词 + benefited from + 负面事件, for + 原因从句

original.en: Only two stray dogs benefited from all this confusion, for they greedily devoured what was left of the cake.
original.zh: 只有两只流浪狗从这一片混乱中得了好处，因为它们贪婪地吃掉了剩下的蛋糕。

imitations.en: Only the second-hand bookstore benefited from the closure of the local library, for a lot of readers came to buy books there.
imitations.zh: 只有二手书店从当地图书馆的关闭中得了好处，因为很多读者都去那里买书。

imitations.en: Only the delivery platforms benefited from the sudden lockdown, for people could only order food and daily goods online.
imitations.zh: 只有外卖平台从突如其来的封控中得了好处，因为人们只能在网上订购食物和日用品。
